title: euphorbia dwyeri
intro: Euphorbia dwyeri, also known as the 'Dyer's Spurge' or 'Muli-muli', is a species of succulent plant native to the tropical forests of Hawaii. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Euphorbia dwyeri include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat various skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in water or oil, and are often used in traditional Hawaiian medicine to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
